titleStmt.title: Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 5969

div.p: To all Christ's faithful to whom the present written document shall come, Hugh Levedyman of Langehope, and Eve his wife, sister of Laurence son of William son of Gocelin of Berwick, send greeting. Know us, all of you, to have granted at the urging of the said Laurence, and have made firm by this our present charter, quitclaimed, and for ourselves and our heirs for ever, entirely abjured the donation and grant of the whole land, without any withholding, in the territory of Prenderguest which Laurence, son of William son of Gocelin of Berwick, had from the gift of Robert his brother, which the same Laurence gave, granted, and by his own charter made firm to God, St Ebbe, Richard the prior, and the convent of Coldingham and their successors, in free, pure, and perpetual alms. We have also pledged and sworn, touching holy relics, for ourselves and our heirs, that we shall in no era of the world seek to move against the aforesaid donation and grant. As witness of this matter, we have applied our seals to the present document. As witnesses, the lord William of Mordington, the lord David of Haddington, the lord Ralph Noble, the lord William of Scremerston, Adam of Prenderguest, Adam son of John of Over Ayton, Thomas of Nisbet, Bertram of Reston, Patrick Scott, Gilbert of Lumsdaine, John son of Ingram, Gregory of Bethock, and others.
